## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about why people don't attend webinars. So this is a huge industry wide problem. People who sign up for webinars simply don't attend them. On average, we only get a five to 30% attendance rate. So that means 70% to 95% of everyone who actually came to a webinar registration page, filled it all out, actually registered for it, don't show up to the webinar itself. So when you look at it, this is probably the single biggest issue in this webinar industry. If we could solve it, all kinds of things will become better. So why not? Let's just fix it. If you look at it, it's the issue with the greatest leverage in our industry. If we could double our attendance rate, we could potentially double ourselves on the other end. As we look at this, and we look at why aren't people attending, it's clear, and it's very true that between the time that someone signs up and the webinar itself, there's lots of things that go on during that period. There's lots of challenges, distractions, everything that comes up that could cause somebody to not attend the webinar, whether it's happening because they're enthusiasm dwindles, or it happens because they just got busy, or hey, life happens because people truly are busy, and people literally do forget about your webinar, and people sometimes overbook themselves, so something's got to go. And then there's always the other factor that there's always the replay. I can just watch that anytime. It's just like TV used to be at seven o'clock on Thursday night. Now TV is whenever you want it. And that has come into this because people will say, well, I don't have to come to your webinar at 4 p.m. I can watch the replay anytime. So when it comes to marketing problems, there are really only two types of solutions. There's a math solution. I just need to get more people to register for the webinar so that I can get more people to come to the webinar so that I can sell more, or I just need to buy more ads or whatever that math solution is. And then there are psychology solutions where I can take the people who signed

up for that webinar, and I can do a better job of causing them to think the way I want them to, so that they'll actually attend. Now here's the thing about marketing. I find people all the time who say, if I could just get more traffic to my webpage, it'll be better. When in reality, in many cases, their problem isn't that they're not getting enough traffic. ## [09:04 - 12:05]

conflicts, they just can't come to a webinar one o'clock in the afternoon on Thursday, because you didn't give enough warning. Now, by the way, just a side journey here for just a moment, many evergreen webinar tools solve this problem by offering an immediate broadcast time option. So the next webinar showing starts in 15 minutes or 30 minutes, trying to get past this day one, day two, day three thing, they just send people right there. Well, that's great if they can attend, but they got a meeting in half an hour, they're not going to attend. So that's not all the solution. And by the way, I do also recommend adding an option to your thank you page for them to be able to watch a replay of our last webinar right now. So even if you're not doing an evergreen webinar, you're doing a regular webinar, I actually recommend that you record a version of that webinar that you can put up as an option to watch a replay of that last webinar right now. So they can see it right then and you take care of that problem for those who actually have the capability of watching it right now. All right. Now, there is another solution that a lot of people do, which is they offer an attendance bonus. So basically, this is a bribe to attend. And sometimes they say in you'll get it at the end. So you have to come to the webinar and stay to the end to get this bonus. The problem with that is that it really doesn't usually work. Most people know that that bonus that you're offering them really isn't worth much. And it's not enough. It worked for a while, like three years ago, but it's not enough now to get people to actually come to the webinar. So the solution that most people are using is they're trying to give better reminders. So they're adding more reminder emails to make sure that people see it. They remember that they should come. They're adding text and SMS and messenger reminders into the equation so that people if they're email, they're not reading their emails or emails are getting blocked, they're seeing those. And they're putting maybe communication both from your auto responder system as well as your webinar company's email system, hoping that those will get delivered and read and so that people will actually come. And don't get me wrong. I do recommend doing this. In fact, I do recommend doing everything we talked about here. The problem is that this does nothing to deal with the skeptical and negative emotions that they're experiencing. They're just getting reminded more. In fact, it actually backfires a bit where people say, these guys keep reminding me, they must really want me to come and buy something from them.

## [12:07 - 15:11]

And so may I recommend that a better solution here is to apply the psychology solution specifically to deal with the skeptical and negative emotions that they're experiencing so they'll actually want to attend. So what are those emotions? The first one is that they have a lack of trust that you're actually going to give them content in the webinar that is good enough that it's worth their time. This is made worse by all the people who are doing bad webinars out there who that the entire webinar is content free. It is simply devoted to getting people to buy. And so we have to combat against that to tell them that our solution is actually better than what everyone else is promising that our webinar will actually give them what we promise them to do. We've got to build trust by demonstrating trustworthiness. And notice I didn't say by telling them that they can trust us. You don't get trust by telling someone they can trust you. Whenever I hear that, I think of a used car salesman saying, trust me, you're going to get a great car here. You don't develop trust by telling people they can trust you. You develop trust by demonstrating that you can be trusted. The next emotion is skepticism. Skepticism that he won't or can't deliver the promised transformation or he or she won't give the solution for free. I'm going to have to buy something to get it. Now this is very related to that trust, but technically it's a separate piece. And the way you solve this is by providing value that moves them towards achieving the transformation immediately between the registration and the webinar. So let me just state that again. You provide them value between the time when they register and the time they actually attend the webinar that enables them to start receiving transformations in their lives. The transformation that you promised to deliver in the webinar, you're giving this prior pieces of it prior to the webinar so that they're walking into the webinar with their skepticism reduced and their trust increased that you are actually going to give them content during that webinar that will deliver on what you promised you'd give them. Now you've got something that really
